purpose: This commit fixes a potential use-after-free bug in Bluetooth HCI synchronization. It ensures hci_cmd_sync_queue returns an error when HCI_UNREGISTER is set, preventing commands from being queued after device unregistration.

The commit removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h from n_hdlc.c. It ensures proper header inclusion by relying on linux/tty.h which already pulls in linux/termios.h, eliminating redundancy and potential confusion.

This commit updates documentation for the 'N/A' store metric in the perf c2c tool. It reflects changes in the report table after adding the 'N/A' metric for store operations, ensuring the documentation matches the implemented functionality.
Fixes vma lock handling during huge page mapping splits and range unmappings. Addresses race conditions and ensures proper lock management during vma duplication and sharing.

This commit adds device tree binding documentation for AVDD and MICVDD supplies used by the rt5682s codec. It ensures these power supplies are properly documented in the device tree schema, aligning with existing usage in sc7180-trogdor-kingoftown.dtsi.
Fixes a resource leak in the Zorro7xx SCSI driver by adding a missing iounmap() call in the remove function, ensuring proper cleanup during device removal.
Removes an unused work field from struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the Mellanox 5 network driver. The change is a cleanup of dead code, improving code clarity and reducing memory footprint.

The commit changes the type of 'key_idx' in 'cfg80211_auth_request' from unsigned to signed. This prevents static analyzer warnings when -1 is assigned to indicate unused keys, without changing functional behavior.
The commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS configuration option in RISC-V defconfigs. It claims no functional change, aligning with savedefconfig's behavior.
The commit introduces a new helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to check if the chip is in CAN-FD mode, replacing an inline check. It improves code clarity and maintainability.

This commit modifies RTC selftests to skip execution when no RTC device is present. It prevents test failures in environments lacking RTC hardware, improving test reliability and avoiding false negatives.

Removes a redundant variable assignment in ALSA echoaudio driver. The variable 'bytes' is assigned but never read, then reassigned in a conditional block. This cleanup eliminates a clang static analysis warning.

This commit allows the rcuscale feature to be built without requiring RCU Tasks. It decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks, reducing kernel size and simplifying tracing operations in CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE=y kernels.
This commit removes a duplicated 'be split' string in the spte.h file within the KVM x86 mmu code. It's a simple typo fix that corrects a redundant wording in a header file.

This commit reverts a change that populated a DCN315 clock table based on DCFCLK. The revert prevents a no-read situation caused by how the PMFW fills the table when DCFCLK states are disabled, avoiding potential underflow issues.
This commit adds device tree binding documentation for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU, including a new compatible string to enable GPU support in the kernel.

Renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp for consistency with other naming conventions in the DW PCIe driver. The change aligns the naming scheme by adding the 'dw_' prefix and '_rp' suffix to match other local entities.
Fixes a Clang compiler warning by correcting the format specifier for a u16 variable in a printf-like function. Changes %hx to %x to match the promoted int type, resolving a type mismatch issue.
This commit fixes a branch range check in MIPS jump_label implementation by casting the upper bound to long for signed comparison, preventing a warning triggered by negative offsets.

This commit removes a specific case statement HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD from the SetHwReg8188EU() function. It simplifies the function by directly calling the underlying function instead of going through a case statement, as part of a broader effort to eliminate unnecessary HAL layer code.

The commit declares non-global symbols as static in arm64 kernel code to fix sparse warnings. It addresses a specific issue where a symbol was not declared as static, causing a warning during kernel compilation.

This commit corrects incorrect interrupt flag specifications in MDSS display device tree bindings. It removes bogus interrupt flags cells from consumer nodes, as the MDSS interrupt provider uses a single cell, making the flags specification invalid.
This commit allocates space for arrays in the struct ctrl_ref to prepare for allowing drivers to change array sizes. It modifies memory allocation for control references in V4L2 subsystem.
Adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs (SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, SM8450) to the Qualcomm socinfo driver, expanding hardware compatibility.
The commit updates the rtl8xxxu WiFi driver to recognize all possible chip cuts (letters A to P) instead of only up to E, preventing 'unknown' output for newer chip versions.

This commit removes the custom software enable count for PRCMU clocks, relying instead on the core's existing software enable count. It simplifies the clock management by eliminating redundant state tracking.

This commit refactors RISC-V memory management code to reduce reliance on CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL preprocessor directives. It groups related #ifdef usage and moves macro definitions closer to their declarations for better code organization and maintainability.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware support in the sm6350-lena device tree, preparing for future I2C node additions. This is a hardware configuration change for a specific ARM64 SoC.
This commit fixes comments in the rzg2l thermal driver, correcting a misleading description, fixing a typo, and aligning with coding style by placing /* on a separate line.

Fixes an infinite loop in the igc driver's release_swfw_sync function. The commit handles the case where acquiring the HW semaphore fails, typically due to hardware surprise removal, by logging an error and exiting gracefully instead of looping infinitely.
The commit updates the pcm3168a ASoC driver to use modern DAI format terminology for clocking, replacing older defines. It is part of a broader effort to modernize the audio subsystem's API.
Removes redundant IS_SWAPFILE hack code that was always defined. The commit eliminates pointless conditional compilation logic, simplifying the NFS implementation.

This commit implements GTP echo response functionality in the kernel. It handles GTP-U packets with echo requests by sending appropriate echo responses, including recovery information elements with restart counters as per GTP specification.

The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate, improving performance by using a more efficient function. It also introduces cpumask_first_zero() to fill a functionality gap.

This commit removes a call to the 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in the brcmfmac driver. The firmware no longer supports this call, causing repeated error messages during STA roaming. The change prevents these errors by stopping the host driver from attempting the unsupported firmware call.
Adds support for a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) for the sof_rt5682 driver, enabling audio codec alc5682 without speaker amplifier, connected via SSP0, following Intel BT offload design.

This commit ensures zerocopy sendmsg operations fail gracefully when the underlying socket protocol does not support zerocopy. It extends a previous patch that already handled zerocopy send requests for unsupported protocols.

This commit fixes a documentation build issue on RHEL 9 and clones where texlive-xecjk is missing. It tests for xeCJK.sty existence before loading it, preventing xelatex errors when building PDF docs for non-CJK content.

This commit modifies the checkpatch script to allow 'case' macros without reporting errors. It addresses a false positive issue where valid case macros were incorrectly flagged, enabling in-kernel drivers to use such macros without warnings.

The commit converts drm/simpledrm from simple-KMS helpers to regular atomic helpers for better architecture and code sharing. It removes empty CRTC helpers and unconditionally runs drm_atomic_helper_check_plane_state(). No functional changes are introduced.
This commit adds a JPEG decode worker interface to enable parallel execution of three hardware units on MT8195. It introduces a new component for handling JPEG decoding tasks concurrently.
The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls and ata_scsi_dump_cdb() from libata, as their functionality is covered by tracepoints and scsi tracing. It improves code clarity and reduces redundancy.
Adds support for zstate during extended vblank in AMD display driver. Enables entering zstate when transitioning to FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO, using extra vblank time for optimization.
Fixes a CFI violation by aligning the hqd_destroy() callback's reset_type parameter type from enum kfd_preempt_type to uint32_t, matching the actual function pointer prototype.
Corrects the 'resets' property name in device tree bindings for STM32 pinctrl. Fixes a typo and adds 'additionalProperties: false' for validation.

The commit silences an array-bounds warning in the ath9k wireless driver caused by GCC 12. It relegates the warning to W=1+ level instead of treating it as an error, without changing functional behavior.

The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss crypto driver. This change simplifies the code by using a more appropriate runtime power management function, which handles the resume and get operations in one call.

This commit reorganizes io_uring file installation and removal logic by moving it into the filetable code section. It changes helper functions to accept a context instead of an io_kiocb, improving code structure and clarity.

This commit splits the G1 and G2 decoder nodes in the device tree bindings for the i.MX8MQ VPU. It reflects that these are independent blocks sharing only a power domain, and updates the bindings to support separate nodes with power-domain controller support.
The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro, replacing it with direct code since it's only used once. This simplifies the code structure and improves maintainability.
